title: Getting started with satbathy
text: satbathy estimates water depth shallow, low-turbidity coastal waters multispectral satellite imagery using empirical log-ratio transform Stumpf, Holderied Sinclair (2003). idea simple: two visible bands (typically blue green) attenuated different rates depth increases, ratio log-transformed reflectance , useful range, linearly related depth. linear relationship calibrated handful situ soundings applied whole image. vignette walks full workflow bundled example dataset, Landsat scene situ bathymetry Mahahual, Mexican Caribbean coast. image holds five surface-reflectance bands named coastal, blue, green, red nir. points carry depth attribute (metres, negative surface).
code: library(satbathy) library(terra)  img  <- rast(system.file("extdata", "example_sr.tif",     package = "satbathy")) pts  <- vect(system.file("extdata", "example_points.gpkg", package = "satbathy")) deep <- vect(system.file("extdata", "example_deepmask.gpkg", package = "satbathy"))  img #> class       : SpatRaster #> size        : 313, 196, 5  (nrow, ncol, nlyr) #> resolution  : 30, 30  (x, y) #> extent      : 423405, 429285, 2063355, 2072745  (xmin, xmax, ymin, ymax) #> coord. ref. : WGS 84 / UTM zone 16N (EPSG:32616) #> source      : example_sr.tif #> names       :   coastal,     blue,     green,      red,       nir #> min values  : -0.062473, -0.05271, -0.030242, -0.03368, -0.019462 #> max values  :  0.130357, 0.192865,  0.243905,  0.17939,  0.100135

title: Getting started with satbathy
text: ratio transform performs best clear water, first practical step pick least turbid dates image time series. turbidity_index() implements index Frohn Autrey (2009), (green + red) / blue; lower values mean clearer water. simply summarise single example scene. practice compute index every date choose minima (example moving-average smoother time series).
code: turb <- turbidity_index(img) global(turb, "mean", na.rm = TRUE) #>                mean #> turbidity 0.8363226

title: Getting started with satbathy
text: water pixels enter model. sensor quality band available, threshold Normalized Difference Water Index works well: Sensor-specific preprocessing. bundled image already surface reflectance, cropped land/cloud masked. Producing image raw archives depends sensor intentionally left outside package, recipe : Landsat 8/9 (Collection 2 L2). Scale SR_B* bands REFLECTANCE_MULT_BAND / REFLECTANCE_ADD_BAND values MTL metadata, build masks QA_PIXEL bit flags (water bit; dilated cloud, cirrus, cloud cloud-shadow bits). Sentinel-2 (L2A). Use Scene Classification Layer (SCL) keep water discard clouds shadows; reflectance B* bands divided quantification value. PlanetScope / SuperDove (L2 SR). reliable water class UDM2 mask, derive water mask NDWI ndwi() / water_mask().
code: wm <- water_mask(img, green = "green", nir = "nir", threshold = 0) img_water <- mask(img, wm, maskvalues = c(FALSE, NA))

title: Getting started with satbathy
text: Sun glint can removed image-based method Hedley et al. (2005), regresses visible band near-infrared optically deep water (supplied polygon mask). low-pass filter reduces residual speckle. steps optional , calm, uniform waters, make little difference.
code: img_gc <- correct_glint(img_water, vis_bands = c("blue", "green"),                         nir_band = "nir", deep_mask = deep) img_gc <- lowpass_filter(img_gc)

title: Getting started with satbathy
text: Darker offshore tones correspond higher ratio values, .e. greater depth.
code: ratio <- log_ratio(img_gc, blue = "blue", green = "green", n = 10000) plot(ratio, main = "Log-ratio (blue/green)")

title: Getting started with satbathy
text: Pair ratio situ depths, decide deep signal reliable. optimal_max_depth() refits model decreasing sequence maximum-depth thresholds: goodness fit rises linear range fills drops saturated (-deep) points included.  scatter makes saturation explicit: beyond penetration limit ratio longer increases depth.  Now fit model valid range inspect :
code: tab <- extract_ratio(ratio, pts, depth_field = "depth")  md <- optimal_max_depth(tab) plot(md$max_depth, md$r2, type = "b",      xlab = "Maximum depth (m)", ylab = "Adjusted R-squared") best <- md$max_depth[which.max(md$r2)] best #> [1] -31 plot(tab$ratio, tab$depth, pch = 20, col = "grey50",      xlab = "Ratio", ylab = "Depth (m)") abline(h = best, lty = 2) fit <- fit_bathymetry(tab, max_depth = best) fit #> <satbathy_model> #>   depth = -2.036 + -6.072 * ratio #>   points: 277   (max_depth = -31 m) #>   adjusted R-squared: 0.239 plot(fit)

title: Getting started with satbathy
text: Apply calibrated model ratio raster obtain depth map:  honest error estimate, hold independent points within valid depth range score evaluate_bathymetry():
code: depth_map <- predict_bathymetry(fit, ratio) plot(depth_map, main = "Satellite-derived bathymetry (m)") valid <- tab[tab$depth > best, ] set.seed(1) i <- sample(nrow(valid), round(0.7 * nrow(valid))) train <- valid[i, ] test  <- valid[-i, ]  fit_tr <- fit_bathymetry(train) evaluate_bathymetry(fit_tr, test) #>    n     rmse      mae #> 1 83 12.25251 4.068439

title: Getting started with satbathy
text: Frohn RC, Autrey BC (2009). Water quality assessment Ohio River using new indices turbidity chlorophyll-Landsat-7 imagery. U.S. EPA. Hedley JD, Harborne AR, Mumby PJ (2005). Simple robust removal sun glint mapping shallow-water benthos. International Journal Remote Sensing 26(10):2107-2112. McFeeters SK (1996). use Normalized Difference Water Index (NDWI) delineation open water features. International Journal Remote Sensing 17(7):1425-1432. Stumpf RP, Holderied K, Sinclair M (2003). Determination water depth high-resolution satellite imagery variable bottom types. Limnology Oceanography 48(1, part 2):547-556.

what: Satellite-Derived Bathymetry with the Empirical Ratio Transform
title: Satellite-Derived Bathymetry with the Empirical Ratio Transform
text: satbathy derives bathymetry (water depth) shallow, low-turbidity coastal waters multispectral satellite imagery — Landsat 8/9, Sentinel-2 PlanetScope/SuperDove — using empirical log-ratio transform Stumpf, Holderied Sinclair (2003). package covers full empirical workflow: turbidity indexing select clear-water dates, water masking, sun-glint correction, spatial filtering, log-ratio transform, linear model calibration prediction, iterative estimation maximum reliable depth, accuracy assessment.

title: Satellite-Derived Bathymetry with the Empirical Ratio Transform
text: Install development version GitHub:
code: # install.packages("pak") pak::pak("jfmas/satbathy")

title: Satellite-Derived Bathymetry with the Empirical Ratio Transform
code: library(satbathy) library(terra)  # Bundled Mahahual (Mexican Caribbean) example img <- rast(system.file("extdata", "example_sr.tif",  package = "satbathy")) pts <- vect(system.file("extdata", "example_points.gpkg", package = "satbathy"))  # 1. Mask land/clouds, keep water only img <- mask(img, water_mask(img), maskvalues = c(FALSE, NA))  # 2. Log-ratio transform (Stumpf et al. 2003) ratio <- log_ratio(img, blue = "blue", green = "green")  # 3. Calibrate against in situ depths tab <- extract_ratio(ratio, pts, depth_field = "depth")  # 4. Find the maximum depth the signal resolves, then fit md  <- optimal_max_depth(tab) fit <- fit_bathymetry(tab, max_depth = md$max_depth[which.max(md$r2)]) fit #> <satbathy_model> #>   depth = ... + ... * ratio  # 5. Predict a depth map and assess accuracy depth_map <- predict_bathymetry(fit, ratio) evaluate_bathymetry(fit, tab)

title: Satellite-Derived Bathymetry with the Empirical Ratio Transform
text: log-ratio transform linearises reflectance–depth relationship related depth simple linear model Z = m1 * R - m0 calibrated situ soundings. strongly attenuated band saturates first, ratio tracks depth penetration limit; optimal_max_depth() locates limit model fitted valid range.
code: R = ln(n * R_blue) / ln(n * R_green)

title: Satellite-Derived Bathymetry with the Empirical Ratio Transform
text: use satbathy, please cite Stumpf et al. (2003) underlying method, : Mas JF, Pérez Vega . Empirical bathymetry satellite images.

title: Satellite-Derived Bathymetry with the Empirical Ratio Transform
text: MIT © Jean François Mas Azucena Pérez Vega.

what: Accuracy metrics for depth predictions — accuracy
title: Accuracy metrics for depth predictions — accuracy
text: rmse() mae() return root-mean-square error mean absolute error observed predicted depths.

title: Accuracy metrics for depth predictions — accuracy
code: rmse(observed, predicted, na.rm = TRUE)  mae(observed, predicted, na.rm = TRUE)

title: Accuracy metrics for depth predictions — accuracy
text: observed Numeric vector observed (situ) depths. predicted Numeric vector predicted depths, length observed. na.rm Logical; TRUE (default) pairs missing value either vector dropped computing metric.

title: Accuracy metrics for depth predictions — accuracy
text: single numeric value.

title: Accuracy metrics for depth predictions — accuracy
text: $$RMSE = \sqrt{\frac{\sum (Z_o - Z_p)^2}{n}}, \qquad       MAE  = \frac{\sum |Z_o - Z_p|}{n}$$

title: Accuracy metrics for depth predictions — accuracy
Sun-glint correction (Hedley et al. 2005) — correct_glint
text: Removes sun-glint visible bands using image-based method Hedley et al. (2005). visible band, linear regression near-infrared (NIR) band fitted deep-water sample, bottom contribution assumed negligible NIR reflectance due glint alone. band corrected band - slope * (NIR - min_NIR), min_NIR glint-free NIR reflectance estimated low quantile NIR deep water.

title: Sun-glint correction (Hedley et al. 2005) — correct_glint
code: correct_glint(   x,   vis_bands = c("blue", "green"),   nir_band = "nir",   deep_mask,   n_sample = 500,   prob = 0.01 )

title: Sun-glint correction (Hedley et al. 2005) — correct_glint
text: x multi-band terra::SpatRaster surface reflectance. vis_bands Band identifiers (layer names integer indices) visible bands correct, e.g. c("blue", "green"). nir_band Band identifier near-infrared band. deep_mask terra::SpatVector (SpatRaster) delimiting optically deep water, used sample glint estimate min_NIR. Reprojected CRS x necessary. n_sample Number random deep-water pixels used fit regression. Defaults 500. prob Lower quantile NIR deep water used estimate glint-free NIR reflectance (min_NIR). Hedley et al. (2005) use minimum NIR value found sample; small quantile (default 0.01) used robust estimate. Set prob = 0 literal minimum.

title: Sun-glint correction (Hedley et al. 2005) — correct_glint
text: copy x layers named vis_bands glint-corrected. deep-water sample small fit regressions, x returned unchanged warning.

title: Sun-glint correction (Hedley et al. 2005) — correct_glint
text: Hedley JD, Harborne AR, Mumby PJ (2005). Technical note: Simple robust removal sun glint mapping shallow-water benthos. International Journal Remote Sensing 26(10):2107-2112. doi:10.1080/01431160500034086

title: Sun-glint correction (Hedley et al. 2005) — correct_glint
code: img  <- terra::rast(system.file("extdata", "example_sr.tif",                                 package = "satbathy")) deep <- terra::vect(system.file("extdata", "example_deepmask.gpkg",                                 package = "satbathy")) corrected <- correct_glint(img, vis_bands = c("blue", "green"),                            nir_band = "nir", deep_mask = deep)

what: Evaluate a bathymetry model against independent data — evaluate_bathymetry
title: Evaluate a bathymetry model against independent data — evaluate_bathymetry
text: Predicts depth test table returns accuracy metrics.

title: Evaluate a bathymetry model against independent data — evaluate_bathymetry
code: evaluate_bathymetry(model, newdata, ratio = "ratio", depth = "depth")

title: Evaluate a bathymetry model against independent data — evaluate_bathymetry
text: model satbathy_model fit_bathymetry(). newdata data.frame ratio observed depth columns (e.g. extract_ratio() held-points). ratio, depth Column names ratio observed depth newdata. Default "ratio" "depth".

title: Evaluate a bathymetry model against independent data — evaluate_bathymetry
text: one-row data.frame columns n, rmse mae.

title: Evaluate a bathymetry model against independent data — evaluate_bathymetry
code: img <- terra::rast(system.file("extdata", "example_sr.tif",                                package = "satbathy")) pts <- terra::vect(system.file("extdata", "example_points.gpkg",                                package = "satbathy")) img <- terra::mask(img, water_mask(img), maskvalues = c(FALSE, NA)) tab <- extract_ratio(log_ratio(img), pts) fit <- fit_bathymetry(tab, max_depth = -16) evaluate_bathymetry(fit, tab) #>     n     rmse      mae #> 1 378 65.26401 31.87794

what: Extract ratio values at in situ depth points — extract_ratio
title: Extract ratio values at in situ depth points — extract_ratio
text: Builds modelling table used calibrate bathymetry model extracting log-ratio value (see log_ratio()) situ bathymetric point pairing measured depth.

title: Extract ratio values at in situ depth points — extract_ratio
code: extract_ratio(ratio, points, depth_field = "depth")

title: Extract ratio values at in situ depth points — extract_ratio
text: ratio single-layer terra::SpatRaster ratio values. points situ bathymetric points, either terra::SpatVector sf object points. Reprojected CRS ratio needed. depth_field Name attribute points holding measured depth (negative surface). Defaults "depth".

title: Extract ratio values at in situ depth points — extract_ratio
text: data.frame columns ratio depth, rows containing missing values removed.

title: Extract ratio values at in situ depth points — extract_ratio
code: img <- terra::rast(system.file("extdata", "example_sr.tif",                                package = "satbathy")) pts <- terra::vect(system.file("extdata", "example_points.gpkg",                                package = "satbathy")) wm  <- water_mask(img) img <- terra::mask(img, wm, maskvalues = c(FALSE, NA)) ratio <- log_ratio(img) tab <- extract_ratio(ratio, pts, depth_field = "depth") head(tab) #>       ratio       depth #> 1 1.2292454 -187.000000 #> 2 1.0426425   -4.696015 #> 3 1.0446726   -4.245674 #> 4 1.0613218   -8.109966 #> 5 1.1346521  -10.282619 #> 6 0.8834699   -3.500000

what: Fit an empirical bathymetry model — fit_bathymetry
title: Fit an empirical bathymetry model — fit_bathymetry
text: Fits empirical linear model relating depth log-ratio, \(Z = m_1 R - m_0\) (Stumpf et al. 2003), ordinary least squares. Optionally restricts calibration points shallower maximum depth, since ratio saturates departs linearity beyond penetration limit (see optimal_max_depth()).

title: Fit an empirical bathymetry model — fit_bathymetry
code: fit_bathymetry(data, ratio = "ratio", depth = "depth", max_depth = NULL)

title: Fit an empirical bathymetry model — fit_bathymetry
text: data data.frame ratio depth columns, typically output extract_ratio(). ratio, depth Column names data ratio measured depth. Default "ratio" "depth". max_depth Optional maximum depth (negative number). points depth > max_depth (depth < 0) used calibration. NULL (default) rows depth < 0 used.

title: Fit an empirical bathymetry model — fit_bathymetry
text: object class satbathy_model: list fitted lm (model), data actually used, max_depth threshold, adjusted R-squared (r.squared), number points (n) call.

title: Fit an empirical bathymetry model — fit_bathymetry
text: Stumpf RP, Holderied K, Sinclair M (2003). Determination water depth high-resolution satellite imagery variable bottom types. Limnology Oceanography 48(1, part 2):547-556. doi:10.4319/lo.2003.48.1_part_2.0547

title: Fit an empirical bathymetry model — fit_bathymetry
code: img <- terra::rast(system.file("extdata", "example_sr.tif",                                package = "satbathy")) pts <- terra::vect(system.file("extdata", "example_points.gpkg",                                package = "satbathy")) img <- terra::mask(img, water_mask(img), maskvalues = c(FALSE, NA)) tab <- extract_ratio(log_ratio(img), pts) fit <- fit_bathymetry(tab, max_depth = -16) fit #> <satbathy_model> #>   depth = 36.43 + -41.53 * ratio #>   points: 259   (max_depth = -16 m) #>   adjusted R-squared: 0.840

what: Interpolate tide-gauge sea level at arbitrary times — interpolate_sea_level
title: Interpolate tide-gauge sea level at arbitrary times — interpolate_sea_level
text: Linearly interpolates tide-gauge sea-level record one target times. used normalise situ echo-sounder depths common datum: sounding referred sea level measured tide gauge moment taken, removing effect tide survey dates.

title: Interpolate tide-gauge sea level at arbitrary times — interpolate_sea_level
code: interpolate_sea_level(   target_time,   df,   time_col = "datetime",   level_col = "nivel" )

title: Interpolate tide-gauge sea level at arbitrary times — interpolate_sea_level
text: target_time Time(s) interpolate sea level. Numeric, Date, POSIXct; vector processed one call. df data.frame holding tide-gauge record. time_col, level_col Names columns df measurement time sea level. Default "datetime" "nivel".

title: Interpolate tide-gauge sea level at arbitrary times — interpolate_sea_level
text: numeric vector, length target_time, interpolated sea level (NA outside record's time range).

title: Interpolate tide-gauge sea level at arbitrary times — interpolate_sea_level
text: function thin, vectorised wrapper around stats::approx() rule = 1, target times outside range record return NA.

title: Interpolate tide-gauge sea level at arbitrary times — interpolate_sea_level
code: gauge <- data.frame(   datetime = as.POSIXct("2025-04-28 13:00", tz = "UTC") +     c(0, 600, 1200, 1800),   nivel = c(5.57, 5.58, 5.60, 5.59) ) # sea level at two sounding times times <- as.POSIXct(c("2025-04-28 13:05", "2025-04-28 13:25"), tz = "UTC") interpolate_sea_level(times, gauge) #> [1] 5.575 5.595

title: Log-ratio transform (Stumpf et al. 2003) — log_ratio
text: Computes log-ratio transform used linearise relationship satellite reflectance water depth: $$R = \frac{\ln(n \cdot R_{blue})}{\ln(n \cdot R_{green})}$$ \(R_{blue}\) \(R_{green}\) reflectance high-penetration lower-penetration band (typically blue green), \(n\) fixed constant chosen logarithms stay positive. strongly attenuated band changes faster depth, ratio increases monotonically depth valid range.

title: Log-ratio transform (Stumpf et al. 2003) — log_ratio
code: log_ratio(x, blue = "blue", green = "green", n = 10000, trim = c(0.01, 0.99))

title: Log-ratio transform (Stumpf et al. 2003) — log_ratio
text: x multi-band terra::SpatRaster surface reflectance. Non-water areas masked beforehand (see water_mask()); cells non-positive reflectance yield NA. blue, green Band identifiers (layer names integer indices) high-penetration (blue) lower-penetration (green) bands. n Fixed positive constant applied inside logarithms, chosen logarithms stay positive. Defaults 10000. Stumpf et al. (2003) report ratio insensitive n (root-mean-square error changing less 0.4 m n 500 1500), rescales ratio, absorbed calibration coefficients. trim Length-2 numeric vector lower/upper probabilities used trim outliers ratio: cells outside quantiles set NA. Defaults c(0.01, 0.99). Use NULL disable trimming.

title: Log-ratio transform (Stumpf et al. 2003) — log_ratio
text: single-layer terra::SpatRaster named "ratio".

title: Log-ratio transform (Stumpf et al. 2003) — log_ratio
text: Stumpf RP, Holderied K, Sinclair M (2003). Determination water depth high-resolution satellite imagery variable bottom types. Limnology Oceanography 48(1, part 2):547-556. doi:10.4319/lo.2003.48.1_part_2.0547

title: Log-ratio transform (Stumpf et al. 2003) — log_ratio
code: img <- terra::rast(system.file("extdata", "example_sr.tif",                                package = "satbathy")) wm  <- water_mask(img, green = "green", nir = "nir") img <- terra::mask(img, wm, maskvalues = c(FALSE, NA)) ratio <- log_ratio(img, blue = "blue", green = "green") ratio #> class       : SpatRaster #> size        : 313, 196, 1  (nrow, ncol, nlyr) #> resolution  : 30, 30  (x, y) #> extent      : 423405, 429285, 2063355, 2072745  (xmin, xmax, ymin, ymax) #> coord. ref. : WGS 84 / UTM zone 16N (EPSG:32616) #> source(s)   : memory #> varname     : example_sr #> name        :    ratio #> min value   : 0.776217 #> max value   : 1.701639

title: Low-pass spatial filter — lowpass_filter
text: Applies low-pass (moving-average) spatial filter every layer raster, replacing cell mean neighbourhood. smooths speckle caused residual glint water-surface roughness computing ratio transform. thin, documented wrapper around terra::focal().

title: Low-pass spatial filter — lowpass_filter
code: lowpass_filter(x, w = 3)

title: Low-pass spatial filter — lowpass_filter
text: x terra::SpatRaster. w Neighbourhood size passed terra::focal(). single odd integer gives w x w window (default 3, .e. 3x3 mean).

title: Low-pass spatial filter — lowpass_filter
text: terra::SpatRaster layers x, smoothed.

title: Low-pass spatial filter — lowpass_filter
code: img <- terra::rast(system.file("extdata", "example_sr.tif",                                package = "satbathy")) smoothed <- lowpass_filter(img[["blue"]], w = 3)

title: Normalized Difference Water Index (NDWI) — ndwi
text: Computes McFeeters' Normalized Difference Water Index, (green - nir) / (green + nir). Water typically yields positive values land clouds yield negative values, NDWI convenient basis building water mask sensor quality band available (e.g. PlanetScope imagery).

title: Normalized Difference Water Index (NDWI) — ndwi
code: ndwi(x, green = "green", nir = "nir")

title: Normalized Difference Water Index (NDWI) — ndwi
text: x multi-band terra::SpatRaster surface reflectance. green, nir Band identifiers (layer names integer indices) green near-infrared bands x.

title: Normalized Difference Water Index (NDWI) — ndwi
text: single-layer terra::SpatRaster named "ndwi".

title: Normalized Difference Water Index (NDWI) — ndwi
text: McFeeters SK (1996). use Normalized Difference Water Index (NDWI) delineation open water features. International Journal Remote Sensing 17(7):1425-1432.

title: Normalized Difference Water Index (NDWI) — ndwi
code: img <- terra::rast(system.file("extdata", "example_sr.tif",                                package = "satbathy")) ndwi(img, green = "green", nir = "nir") #> class       : SpatRaster #> size        : 313, 196, 1  (nrow, ncol, nlyr) #> resolution  : 30, 30  (x, y) #> extent      : 423405, 429285, 2063355, 2072745  (xmin, xmax, ymin, ymax) #> coord. ref. : WGS 84 / UTM zone 16N (EPSG:32616) #> source(s)   : memory #> varname     : example_sr #> name        :        ndwi #> min value   : -125.438593 #> max value   :  125.763004

what: Estimate the maximum reliable depth — optimal_max_depth
title: Estimate the maximum reliable depth — optimal_max_depth
text: Determines deep log-ratio keeps linear relationship depth, .e. maximum depth satellite signal can resolve saturates. calibration repeated decreasing sequence maximum-depth thresholds; threshold linear model fitted points shallower goodness fit recorded. adjusted R-squared typically increases linear range included drops saturated (-deep) points added, revealing usable depth limit.

title: Estimate the maximum reliable depth — optimal_max_depth
code: optimal_max_depth(   data,   depths = NULL,   min_n = 10,   ratio = "ratio",   depth = "depth" )

title: Estimate the maximum reliable depth — optimal_max_depth
text: data data.frame ratio depth columns, typically extract_ratio(). depths Optional numeric vector maximum-depth thresholds (negative values). NULL (default) sequence -10 deepest observed point, 1 m steps, used. min_n Minimum number points required fit model given threshold. Thresholds fewer points yield NA metrics. Defaults 10. ratio, depth Column names data. Default "ratio", "depth".

title: Estimate the maximum reliable depth — optimal_max_depth
text: data.frame one row per threshold columns max_depth, n (points used), r2 (adjusted R-squared) rmse (-sample root-mean-square error).

title: Estimate the maximum reliable depth — optimal_max_depth
code: img <- terra::rast(system.file("extdata", "example_sr.tif",                                package = "satbathy")) pts <- terra::vect(system.file("extdata", "example_points.gpkg",                                package = "satbathy")) img <- terra::mask(img, water_mask(img), maskvalues = c(FALSE, NA)) tab <- extract_ratio(log_ratio(img), pts) md <- optimal_max_depth(tab) md[which.max(md$r2), ] #>    max_depth   n        r2     rmse #> 10       -19 266 0.8510812 1.685461

what: Predict depth from a ratio raster or table — predict_bathymetry
title: Predict depth from a ratio raster or table — predict_bathymetry
text: Applies fitted fit_bathymetry() model new ratio values obtain predicted depths. newdata raster result depth map.

title: Predict depth from a ratio raster or table — predict_bathymetry
code: predict_bathymetry(model, ratio)

title: Predict depth from a ratio raster or table — predict_bathymetry
text: model satbathy_model fit_bathymetry(). ratio single-layer terra::SpatRaster ratio values (layer treated ratio predictor).

title: Predict depth from a ratio raster or table — predict_bathymetry
text: single-layer terra::SpatRaster named "depth".

title: Predict depth from a ratio raster or table — predict_bathymetry
code: img <- terra::rast(system.file("extdata", "example_sr.tif",                                package = "satbathy")) pts <- terra::vect(system.file("extdata", "example_points.gpkg",                                package = "satbathy")) img <- terra::mask(img, water_mask(img), maskvalues = c(FALSE, NA)) r   <- log_ratio(img) fit <- fit_bathymetry(extract_ratio(r, pts), max_depth = -16) depth_map <- predict_bathymetry(fit, r)

what: satbathy: Satellite-Derived Bathymetry with the Empirical Ratio Transform — satbathy-package
title: satbathy: Satellite-Derived Bathymetry with the Empirical Ratio Transform — satbathy-package
text: Tools estimate water depth shallow, low-turbidity coastal waters multispectral satellite imagery (e.g. Landsat, Sentinel-2 PlanetScope) using empirical log-ratio transform Stumpf, Holderied Sinclair (2003) doi:10.4319/lo.2003.48.1_part_2.0547 . package provides water masking, sun-glint correction, spatial filtering, log-ratio computation, linear model fitting prediction, iterative estimation maximum reliable depth, turbidity indexing image selection, accuracy assessment.

title: satbathy: Satellite-Derived Bathymetry with the Empirical Ratio Transform — satbathy-package

title: Methods for satbathy_model objects — satbathy_model-methods
text: Printing, summary, prediction plotting methods model returned fit_bathymetry().

title: Methods for satbathy_model objects — satbathy_model-methods
code: # S3 method for class 'satbathy_model' print(x, ...)  # S3 method for class 'satbathy_model' summary(object, ...)  # S3 method for class 'satbathy_model' predict(object, newdata, ...)  # S3 method for class 'satbathy_model' plot(x, ...)

title: Methods for satbathy_model objects — satbathy_model-methods
text: x, object satbathy_model fit_bathymetry(). ... Passed underlying method (e.g. graphical parameters plot). newdata data.frame ratio column, single-layer terra::SpatRaster ratio values. missing, -sample fitted values returned.

title: Methods for satbathy_model objects — satbathy_model-methods
text: print() returns x invisibly; summary() returns lm summary; predict() returns numeric vector (data.frame) terra::SpatRaster (raster); plot() returns NULL invisibly.

title: Turbidity index (Frohn and Autrey 2009) — turbidity_index
text: Computes band-ratio turbidity index Frohn Autrey (2009), defined (green + red) / blue. Higher values indicate turbid water. useful selecting least turbid image dates deriving bathymetry, empirical ratio transform performs best clear water.

title: Turbidity index (Frohn and Autrey 2009) — turbidity_index
code: turbidity_index(x, blue = "blue", green = "green", red = "red")

title: Turbidity index (Frohn and Autrey 2009) — turbidity_index
text: x multi-band terra::SpatRaster surface reflectance. blue, green, red Band identifiers (layer names integer indices) blue, green red bands x.

title: Turbidity index (Frohn and Autrey 2009) — turbidity_index
text: single-layer terra::SpatRaster named "turbidity".

title: Turbidity index (Frohn and Autrey 2009) — turbidity_index
text: Frohn RC, Autrey BC (2009). Water quality assessment Ohio River using new indices turbidity chlorophyll-Landsat-7 imagery. Draft Internal Report, U.S. Environmental Protection Agency.

title: Turbidity index (Frohn and Autrey 2009) — turbidity_index
code: img <- terra::rast(system.file("extdata", "example_sr.tif",                                package = "satbathy")) turb <- turbidity_index(img, blue = "blue", green = "green", red = "red") turb #> class       : SpatRaster #> size        : 313, 196, 1  (nrow, ncol, nlyr) #> resolution  : 30, 30  (x, y) #> extent      : 423405, 429285, 2063355, 2072745  (xmin, xmax, ymin, ymax) #> coord. ref. : WGS 84 / UTM zone 16N (EPSG:32616) #> source(s)   : memory #> varname     : example_sr #> name        :   turbidity #> min value   : -467.441558 #> max value   : 2862.661795

title: Water mask from NDWI — water_mask
text: Builds logical water mask thresholding NDWI (see ndwi()). Pixels NDWI > threshold flagged water (TRUE).

title: Water mask from NDWI — water_mask
code: water_mask(x, green = "green", nir = "nir", threshold = 0)

title: Water mask from NDWI — water_mask
text: x multi-band terra::SpatRaster surface reflectance. green, nir Band identifiers (layer names integer indices) green near-infrared bands x. threshold Numeric NDWI threshold pixel considered water. Defaults 0.

title: Water mask from NDWI — water_mask
text: single-layer logical terra::SpatRaster named "water" (TRUE water, FALSE otherwise).

title: Water mask from NDWI — water_mask
code: img <- terra::rast(system.file("extdata", "example_sr.tif",                                package = "satbathy")) wm <- water_mask(img, green = "green", nir = "nir", threshold = 0) # keep only water pixels of the image: water_only <- terra::mask(img, wm, maskvalues = c(FALSE, NA))

title: satbathy 0.0.0.9000
text: turbidity_index() — turbidity index Frohn & Autrey (2009) selecting least turbid image dates. ndwi() / water_mask() — NDWI water masking (McFeeters 1996). correct_glint() — sun-glint correction (Hedley et al. 2005). lowpass_filter() — low-pass spatial filter. log_ratio() — log-ratio transform (Stumpf et al. 2003). extract_ratio(), fit_bathymetry(), predict_bathymetry(), evaluate_bathymetry() — model calibration, prediction assessment, S3 satbathy_model class (print/summary/predict/plot). optimal_max_depth() — iterative estimation maximum reliable depth (signal-saturation analysis). rmse() / mae() — accuracy metrics. interpolate_sea_level() — tide-gauge sea-level interpolation normalise situ soundings common datum. Bundled Mahahual (Mexican Caribbean) example dataset inst/extdata.
